name: PALESTINE CHILDRENS RELIEF FUND

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows high alignment with donor intent through its ZAKAT-ELIGIBLE status and clear focus on global health urgency [2]. With a fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised, PCRF maximizes the impact of every donation, supporting a track record of treating thousands of children through volunteer medical missions and community-centered projects [5][9].
dimension_explanations.impact: PCRF demonstrates high effectiveness by maintaining a program expense ratio of 88.1%, ensuring the vast majority of its $94,873,840 in total revenue directly funds medical missions and food security projects [1][3]. The organization provides specialized interventions and critical infrastructure support, backed by 40.9 months of working capital which ensures long-term sustainability for its healthcare capacity-building programs [4][7].

baseline_narrative.summary: Founded to address the urgent health needs of children in conflict zones, the Palestine Children's Relief Fund (PCRF) operates through medical missions, infrastructure enhancement, and direct humanitarian relief [5][6]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, maintaining a dedicated fund to support eligible categories of beneficiaries in accordance with Islamic principles [2].

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects verified outcome tracking of <cite id="6">thousands of medical interventions</cite> and food distributions across multiple annual reports.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: PCRF reduces dependency on outside providers by <cite id="4">training local doctors and building hospital infrastructure</cite> while providing immediate surgical care.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that specialized medical missions translate into long-term survival and health for pediatric patients.

strengths.detail: The organization operates with a <cite id="1">88.1% program expense ratio</cite> and spends only <cite id="1">$0.02 to raise every dollar</cite>, ensuring maximum donor impact.
PCRF holds a <cite id="4">Candid Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and maintains <cite id="1">88.1% independent board members</cite>.
The charity maintains <cite id="1">40.9 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, providing immense stability during regional crises.

strengths_deep_dive: PCRF's operational strength lies in its ability to mobilize specialized medical expertise at scale. In 2025, the organization reported <cite id="7">1,946 specialized medical interventions</cite> and distributed <cite id="7">52,449 food parcels and vegetable baskets</cite>. Their financial health is bolstered by a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 106.3%</cite>, demonstrating a massive expansion in donor trust and capacity to absorb funding during humanitarian emergencies.
rich_narrative.summary: The Palestine Children's Relief Fund (PCRF) serves as a critical lifeline for pediatric healthcare in the Middle East, focusing on providing <cite id="5">free medical care and humanitarian aid</cite> to children regardless of political or religious affiliation. The organization operates through a sophisticated model of <cite id="6">volunteer medical missions</cite>, infrastructure development, and direct surgical sponsorships. In 2023, the charity experienced a massive surge in scale, reporting <cite id="3">$92,196,846 in total revenue</cite>, a significant increase from previous years driven by urgent humanitarian needs. Despite this rapid growth, PCRF maintained exceptional fiscal discipline with a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 88.1%</cite> and a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised</cite>. Their impact is tangible and multi-faceted; in 2023 alone, they facilitated <cite id="6">1,074 children treated in medical missions</cite> and provided <cite id="6">196,650 meals via ANERA cooperation</cite>. Beyond emergency relief, PCRF invests in long-term health infrastructure, such as the <cite id="5">Huda Al Masri Pediatric Cancer Department</cite>, to build local capacity. The organization is also a primary choice for Muslim donors, offering a <cite id="2">verified Zakat Fund</cite> that directs 88.1% of eligible donations to the poor and needy (masakin) in accordance with Islamic principles.
